title: Caretaker stage A — derived-fact auto-recompute (birthday → age, etc.)
adr_required: ADR-007 — memory lifecycle (stated → derived → invalidated → garbage-collected)
current_state: Pi's L3 is append-only. 'User is 19' sits there forever, even after the user turns 20. 'User is 19' and 'User born 2006-08-17' coexist as independent facts with no derivation link. Near-duplicate L3 entries (cosine > 0.9) are not merged. Contradicting writes don't invalidate the older record.
structural_problem: Without a maintenance loop, memory rots: derived facts go stale, redundancy compounds, contradictions accumulate. T-078 added invalid_at and T-080 added embedding dedup as one-off jobs. A caretaker generalises both into a continuous process.
target_state: agent/caretaker.py exports CaretakerJob with ONE mode (lite). Derived facts marked {kind: 'derived', source_id: <orig>, recompute_after: <iso>, formula: 'age_from_birthday'|...}. Runs per-bubble (cheap, idempotent) + daily cron. Recomputes any derived fact whose recompute_after has passed. ADR-007 documents the lifecycle (this stage only — stages B and C follow in T-125b and T-125c).
files_affected: docs/adr/007-memory-lifecycle.md (NEW — lifecycle skeleton, full ADR completed in T-125c)
memory/pipeline.py — recompute hook on derived-fact write
tools/tools_memory.py — memory_write detects derivable facts (birthday → age)
agent/session.py — caretaker.lite() as session-exit step (in addition to bubble triggers)
design_notes: Derived fact schema extension: {kind: 'derived', source_id: str, recompute_after: iso, formula: 'age_from_birthday'|'days_until_date'|'days_since_date'}
Formula registry: agent/caretaker.py:_FORMULAS — pure functions (source_value, now) → new_content
Lite mode only: walk L3 WHERE kind='derived' AND recompute_after < now; recompute each, update content + recompute_after
All mutations SOFT — never DELETE, only update content + recompute_after
Concurrency: filelock data/caretaker.lock so cron and session-exit/bubble don't collide
Idempotency: calling lite() repeatedly is safe
Backfill: scan existing L3 once for born-YYYY-MM-DD patterns; spawn derived 'age' fact for each found
success_criteria: User profile contains 'User born 2006-08-17' and a derived 'User is 19 (as of 2026-05-24, recompute after 2026-08-17)'
After 2026-08-17, derived fact auto-updates to 'User is 20'
Lite mode is cheap enough to run on every bubble close (<50ms)
Filelock prevents bubble + cron from racing
ADR-007 lifecycle skeleton documents at minimum: stated, derived, recompute_after, invalid_at
risk_notes: Correctness risk: bad formula updates wrong fact. Mitigation: all updates wrapped in dry-run mode for first deploy; soft invalidation only. Performance risk: nightly deep mode could be expensive on large L3. Mitigation: batch by category; bound to N facts per run.
migration_plan: 1. Write ADR-007 — lifecycle definition first, then code follows the doc
2. Write agent/caretaker.py with three modes + formula registry
3. Add derived-fact write path in memory_write (detect 'born YYYY-MM-DD' → spawn derived 'age')
4. Add session-exit step (uses existing EXIT_STEPS infra from T-085)
6. Tests: synthetic L3 with derived facts; time-jump fixtures
7. Backfill: one-shot script to scan existing L3 for missed derivables
